homework.title: Identifying Rational Numbers
homework.instructions: Select the correct option that represents a rational number.
homework.question: Which of the following is a rational number?
homework.answerSpace: Write the correct option letter here: ____

homework.title: Properties of Rational Numbers
homework.instructions: Fill in the blanks with appropriate terms based on the properties of rational numbers.
homework.question: The sum of two rational numbers is always a ______ number. The product of two non-zero rational numbers is always a ______ number.

homework.title: Representing Rational Numbers on a Number Line
homework.instructions: Draw a number line and mark the position of the given rational numbers. Show your steps for finding the positions.

question.text: Represent the following rational numbers on a number line:

homework.answerSpace: Draw your number line in the space provided below.
homework.visualRequirement: Draw a horizontal line with arrows at both ends. Mark integers from -2 to 3. Divide segments into appropriate parts to locate -1.5 and 1.25.
homework.expectedResponse: A correctly drawn number line showing -1.5 (between -1 and -2) and 1.25 (between 1 and 2).

homework.title: Difference between Rational and Irrational Numbers
homework.instructions: Explain the difference between rational and irrational numbers with one example of each.
homework.question: What is the main difference between a rational number and an irrational number? Give one example of each.
homework.expectedResponse: A rational number can be expressed as p/q where p and q are integers and q≠0 (e.g., 1/2). An irrational number cannot be expressed in this form and has a non-terminating, non-repeating decimal expansion (e.g., √2).

homework.title: Rational Numbers in Daily Life
homework.instructions: Identify rational numbers in the given real-life scenarios and explain why they are rational.
homework.question: A recipe requires 3/4 cup of sugar. A car travels 150 km using 12 liters of fuel. Identify the rational numbers in these statements and explain why they are rational.
homework.expectedResponse: 3/4 is rational because it is a ratio of integers. 150/12 (or 12.5) is rational because it can be expressed as a fraction of integers.

homework.title: Conceptual Understanding of Rational Numbers
homework.instructions: Read the Assertion (A) and Reason (R) carefully. Choose the correct option.
homework.question: Assertion (A): Every integer is a rational number. Reason (R): An integer can be expressed in the form p/q where q is not zero.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
homework.expectedResponse: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.

learningOutcomesCovered: LO_01: Define and identify rational numbers
LO_02: Represent rational numbers on a number line and apply closure properties
LO_03: Apply rational numbers in real-life contexts
